text.title: Anti-Pattern Rationale Template Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Anti-Pattern Rationale Template. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Each Anti-Pattern Rationale Template artefact captures exactly one instance; batched records that conflate multiple instances are rejected.
rule.rationale: Batching turns a 1-decision artefact into a debate club; per-instance records keep rationale traceable and stale-detection accurate.

rule.statement: Every artefact carries at least one evidence link (URL or repo path) pointing at the user-behaviour data; prose-only evidence is rejected.
rule.rationale: Untraceable evidence makes the artefact unauditable; future readers cannot verify the claim or detect drift.

rule.statement: Owner field is a named handle, email, or role-with-rotation; pronouns (we, us, team) are rejected.
rule.rationale: Plural ownership = no ownership; future readers need a named human to chase when the artefact goes stale.

rule.statement: Artefact carries semver version and ISO last_reviewed date; stale records (>90 days unreviewed) must surface a warning on read.
rule.rationale: Decision artefacts decay; treating them as immutable produces folklore. Versioning + dated review force a refresh cadence.

rule.statement: Rationale section names at least one input (Prerequisites table row) by name; rationales without a sourced citation are rejected.
rule.rationale: Ungrounded rationales are post-hoc justifications that drift from the real evidence; named citations are auditable.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do NOT run this methodology — emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition and route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above for flat methodologies), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-anti-pattern-rationale-template.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
